text: Lisia aki …ĒsaŐÉ si banama gi liboet…Ē lil…õ niŐĀ ba awla niŐĀ y…õ su i, baba w…Ē kl…Ē. Xe wo či onyime li…õ ya igbeŐÄ e, w…Ē tut…Ē w…Ēam…Ē eboa la gi la kwik…Ē niŐĀ y…õ su i.‚ÄĚ

ref: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
text: Yudan…õ ma ky…õ b…õtr…Ē niŐĀ y…õ liboe le a ča si lil…õ niŐĀ l…õ m…õ. Paulo AŇčwl…õ Isu Litukpo niŐĀ Felikisi aŇču m…õ

ref: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
text: Gi Kesea …Ē čadi ye abasi Paulo awla la si …Ēpani i, Paulo apani si: ‚ÄúMete si wonu otsixwa ni kesea lia a ča ag…Ē glegle la ya, l…õ loso linu m…õ isami si miŇčwl…õ isu litukpo niŐĀ w…Ē aŇču m…õ.

ref: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
text: Ňäwisi ewe li…Ēf…Ē ni ta ča ko la ya gi matr…õ niŐĀ Yerusalem si mizeesumu Aya a. Ban…Ē biatani likla kutr…Ē l…õ a ča kiw…Ē …Ē,

ref: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
text: Banama gi b…õk…Ē liboe niŐĀ m…õ su i, baŐĀm…Ē si m…õ ni …Ēn…Ēn…Ē kwihe eboe niŐĀ lis…Ēligba l…õ m…õ …õ, loo si m…õ…õpisa …Ēn…Ēha si …Ēy…Ē kedu niŐĀ …Ēn…Ēn…Ē su u, niŐĀ Yudan…õ ma …Ētsak…Ē loo niŐĀ …Ēk…Ē dzedze …Ēt…Ēt…Ē k…Ē niŐĀ …Ēma  čidi no me.

ref: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
text: L…õ baŐĀtratani likla kutr…Ē niŐĀ eboa la gi la b…õk…Ē niŐĀ m…õ su i a ča.

ref: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
text: Mazi l…õ niŐĀ w…Ē aŇču m…õ si meesumu Aya bl…Ē bakatsi ba Aya gani kude uli…Ē gi b…õ…õxwa si ba čaziŇča ba kudeo o, gi b…õ…õsi siŐÄ kup…õ …õ a ča. P…ĒŐÄ m…õ f…õ mazi kid…Ē kakaa a gi b…õŇčwlimi kp…õ niŐĀ Mose …ĒsaŐÉ n…Ē, ani beboedopl…õ ba kuplekpaa m…õ a ča aŇča a.

ref: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
text: Kudeonu didi uli…Ē gi kuli niŐĀ m…õ awla …õ, k…Ē ke kuli niŐĀ ban…Ēaya f…õ ba awla, si …Ēn…Ē kakaa; ban…Ē gbaŐÉ ni ban…Ē zimizi ba p…ĒŐĀ biay…Ē d…Ēni bekpo  ča m…õ …õ.

ref: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
text: L…õ loso mizeekeni idi lipoe kakaa si t…Ē mikiŐĀta kese niŐĀ Aya niŐÄ …Ēn…Ē gbagbe aŇču m…õ …õ.

ref: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
text: ‚ÄúGi mas…õ niŐĀ Yerusalem ag…Ē glegle onu loso e, medzi atr…õ niŐĀkl…Ē ni kib…Ē kiŐĀ m…õ ak…Ēan…õ ma ani t…Ē miz…õ…õki Aya bid…Ē p…Ē či p…Ē či i.

ref: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
text: Meblo ni isu ya kuploploo …Ēk…Ē l…Ē xe b…õm…Ē m…õ niŐĀ lis…Ēligba l…õ m…õ …õ. ∆Ün…Ēha …Ēt…Ēt…Ē oŐĀze p…Ēni m…õ …õ, l…õ ozi loo avit…õ ky…õ biŐĀze nikl…Ē …Ē.

ref: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
text: P…ĒŐÄ Yudan…õ at…Ē gi b…õd…Ē Asia …õ, beze niŐĀkl…Ē, nya lil…õ si beze niŐĀ ya t…Ē bak…Ē ba kunutsatsa akiŐĀ w…Ē ba tut…Ē, xe gi liboet…Ē lil…õ niŐĀ ba awla ni m…õ …õ.

ref: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
text: Loo banama gi bali niŐĀ ya abla …õ, bapani niŐĀ lip…Ē…õ l…õ gi maŇča lipoe l…õ gi mal…õ niŐĀ Yudan…õ ma betsixwa ba aŇču m…õ …õ.

ref: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, ‚ÄúI am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!‚ÄĚ
text: Sani liboe tile gi me muni m…õ ole lo o, xe mapani i, gi mal…õ niŐĀ ba aŇču m…õ …õ, l…õ linu si: ‚Äėml…õ…õxwa litsi ni m…õ omono k…Ēkp…õ m…õ kuziŇčaa si bekpo ba biay…Ē gi biadzi ligbaŐÉŐĀ a.‚Äô ‚ÄĚ

ref: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, ‚ÄúI will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.‚ÄĚ
text: L…õ Felikisi gi ete ba čaziŇčaa ba kudeo kpaŇču i, apani si ‚ÄúXegi Lisia, baguta ba …Ēka  čidi ye aba …õ, maatsa ml…Ē liboe le e.‚ÄĚ L…õ aki si b…õy…Ē …õ.

ref: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
text: Akp…õ …ĒsaŐÉ aki baguta ba …Ēka…õ gi eepe ye e, si odzini ye …Ēkp…õ ligba l…õ, p…ĒŐÄ t…Ē …Ēki kudeo onu si y…õ ba čian…õ ba biatani y…õ baziazia  ča kubit…õ …Ēki y…õ. Paulo ebu y…õ liboe le ese aki Felikisi ni Drusila

ref: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
text: Ewe at…Ē kede e, Felikisi ni yedze Drusila gi onu Yudan…õ e, b…õba l…õ b…õkp…õ ban…Ē niŐĀ Paulo ese t…Ē benu y…õ eboa la gi …õ…õpani niŐĀ kuziŇča niŐĀ Kristo Yesu su i.

ref: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, ‚ÄúThat's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.‚ÄĚ
text: P…ĒŐÄ gi Paulo az…õ…õpani niŐĀ ligbaŐÉŐĀ gbaŐÉ kuzeze, isu a ča kuŇčaŇča ni litsixwaxwa …õn…õ gi litraŐĀba su i, Lifu liku Felikisi l…õ asi y…õ si, ‚ÄúW…Ēatani kus…õ abla, xegi mam…Ē lipoe l…õ kede e, maxwa w…Ē dz…õ.‚ÄĚ

ref: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
text: Gi azeegyo le edi si Paulo aaki y…õ kib…Ē kit…Ē …õ, azaaxwa y…õ l…õ y…õ ni y…õ bezegu kunugu yo lipoe me ni lipoe me e.

ref: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
text: Agi ag…Ē ta ča …õza …õ, bebu Felikisi is…õn…Ē l…õ b…õmani P…Ērsus Festo niŐĀ y…õ linyi ne me e. ∆Ükp…õ gi Felikisi ap…õ si yip…õ lipo me …õki Yudan…õ ma loso e, aki si Paulo aw…Ē ligba l…õ.

ref: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
text: Ni ewetata kede gi Festo aba eku y…õ kesea m…õ …õ, aŐĀd…Ē Kaisarea l…õ emu atr…õ niŐĀ Yerusalem i

ref: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
text: nikl…Ē kusumu yo baka  čidi ba ni Yudan…õ ma …Ēt…Ē n…Ē bezeze ba b…õz…õtsaŐÄ Paulo onu. B…õki Festo litukpo

ref: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
text: si …Ēp…õ lipo me …Ēki ba t…Ē bamani Paulo niŐĀ Yerusalem, l…õ ese linu si b…õbit…õ kunyanya si biaye Paulo niŐĀ kudeo a ča a.

ref: But Festus told them, ‚ÄúPaul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
text: Festo ebu le onu si, ‚ÄúB…õkp…õ Paulo niŐĀ ligba niŐĀ Kaisarea, l…õ m…õ …Ētut…Ē matradzi itr…õ nikl…Ē kpeŐĀkpe e.

ref: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.‚ÄĚ
text: L…õ loso mlaki t…Ē ml…Ē …Ēt…Ē n…Ē bezeze ba bekuni m…õ kude atr…õ niŐĀ Kaisarea at…Ē baz…õtsaŐÄ Paulo onu, xe kesetata eboe at…Ē al…õ niŐĀ ml…Ē awla niŐĀ y…õ su i.‚ÄĚ

ref: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
text: Festo eze niŐĀ ba kusi ewe …Ēg…Ēta ča k…Ētsini li…Ēf…Ē, l…õ kede e edzi eple atr…õ niŐĀ Kaisarea. Kele titia m…õ …õ, Festo azeeze niŐĀ y…õ litsixwagbo le a ča l…õ aki …ĒsaŐÉ si bagbani Paulo aman…Ē …Ē.

ref: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
text: Agi Paulo aba eku i, Yudan…õ a ma gi b…õd…Ē Yerusalem ba …õ, baz…õl…õ…õ klani y…õ l…õ bekpese eboe gagla la kuk…Ē niŐĀ y…õ su i, gi aŐĀtani y…õ kuvu u.

ref: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, ‚ÄúI have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.‚ÄĚ
text: Paulo aŇčwl…õ isu ya litukpo si, ‚ÄúMaŐĀbit…õ kidid…Ē …Ē, gi kiy…Ē kedu niŐĀ Yudan…õ ma ∆ÜsaŐÉ n…Ē su loo niŐĀ Lis…Ēligba l…õ su loo ni ∆Ü čaŇča…õ Kaisaro su u.‚ÄĚ

ref: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, ‚ÄúAre you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?‚ÄĚ
text: Gi Festo ap…õ si yiku Yudan…õ ma liŇču i, l…õ loso e e či Paulo si, ‚ÄúW…Ēap…õ si wutr…õ ni Yerusalem at…Ē miz…õ…õxwa litsi ni w…Ē niŐĀ eboa la gi b…õk…Ē niŐĀ w…Ē su niŐĀ m…õ aŇču m…õ na a?‚ÄĚ

ref: Paul replied, ‚ÄúI am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
text: L…õ Paulo asi Festo si, ‚ÄúOwo mal…õ…õ niŐĀ Kaisaro …Ētut…Ē y…õ otsixwa kl…Ē, nikl…Ē gi biaxwa litsi ni me e. Ma čiŐĀta Yudan…õ ma ese e, kili…õ gi w…Ē tut…Ē wote m…Ēm…Ēm…Ē …õ.

ref: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.‚ÄĚ
text: MaŐĀta …ĒsaŐÉ n…Ē ese e, p…ĒŐÄ xe maŐĀbit…õ kidit…Ē …Ē, gi linu si mitse e, meŐĀ či či si mise niŐĀ b…õ su. P…ĒŐÄ xe anukwar…õ …Ēt…Ēt…Ē …ĒŐĀma niŐĀ eboa la gi b…õk…Ē niŐĀ m…õ su m…õ …õ, …Ēn…Ēn…Ē …ĒŐĀtratani m…õ kuk…Ē …Ēki ba a. Me či si ∆Ü čaŇča…õ Kaisaro …Ētut…Ē …Ēxwa litsi ni me.‚ÄĚ

ref: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, ‚ÄúYou have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!‚ÄĚ Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
text: L…õ gi Festo adz…õ agyina ni y…õ ax…Ēt…Ē ba p…ĒŐÄ …õ, apani si, ‚ÄúAgi w…Ēk…Ē w…Ē eboe la kp…õ niŐĀ Kaisaro iwla l…õ m…õ …õ, k…Ē Kaisaro kl…Ē ko w…Ēatr…õ.‚ÄĚ B…õ gbani Paulo mani Okusi ye Agripa ani Bernike aŇču m…õ

ref: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
text: NiŐĀ ewe at…Ē kede e, Okusi ye Agripa ani Bernike b…õba Kaisarea si b…õ…õpani Festo t…Ē b…õ…õkp…õ y…õ wolaa.

ref: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
text: Agi b…õtraŐĀze niŐĀkl…Ē ewe glegle onu i, Festo apani niŐĀ Paulo su aki Okusi ye Agripa si, ‚ÄúOnyime …Ēt…Ē …Ēl…õ niŐĀ ya gi Felikisi ayur…Ē akp…õ ligba l…õ …õ;

ref: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
text: l…õ agi madzi Yerusalem i, Yudan…õ ma kusumu yo baka  čidi ba ni ban…Ē tsitsi ba b…õtsaŐÄ y…õ onu, l…õ be či si mibu ye lip…Ē …Ē.

ref: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
text: ‚ÄúP…ĒŐÄ masi ba si, bl…Ē Roma ban…õ ma k…Ē …õ, liŐĀma bl…Ē …ĒsaŐÉ n…Ē onu si kuyur…Ē …Ēn…Ē …Ēk…Ē si ata kese e, xe …ĒŐĀtsani banama gi b…õk…Ē liboe niŐĀ y…õ su aŇču m…õ ni aŇču m…õ …õ, t…Ē …Ēm…Ē lipoe at…Ē obu isu ya onu niŐĀ eboa la gi b…õk…Ē ni y…õ su i su.

ref: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
text: Agi b…õba ni ya …õ, meŐĀzizi lipoe l…õ niŐĀ kele titia m…õ …õ, meze niŐĀ m…õ litsixwagbo le a ča l…õ makp…õ ban…Ē si bagbani y…õ aman…Ē …Ē.

ref: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
text: Banama gi b…õy…Ē kedu ya niŐĀ y…õ su i, b…õy…Ē p…ĒŐÄ …õ, baŐĀtsa y…õ onu ni eboe zimizi gi mebu si biapani i.

ref: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
text: Eboa la gi b…õman…Ē ko eze eboe hehe niŐĀ y…õ niŐĀ ba kusumu yo su ani …Ēka …Ēt…Ē gi b…õxwa si Yesu u, gi etse koko o, p…ĒŐÄ xe Paulo k…Ē az…õ l…õ si …Ēl…õ ligbaŐÉŐĀ n…õ su u.

ref: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
text: Agi meŐĀte kili…õ gi maaga niŐĀ liboe zizi n…õ ya su i, me či Paulo xe aap…õ si yitr…õ …õ, niŐĀ Yerusalem at…Ē baz…õxwa litsi ni y…õ niŐĀ eboa la gi b…õk…Ē …õtsa y…õ onu su u.

ref: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
text: P…ĒŐÄ Paulo si y…õ atr…õ niŐĀ Kaisaro kl…Ē …Ē, …Ēl…õ e či si bak…Ē y…õ akp…õ niŐĀ ban…Ēpe ba iwla m…õ …õ ani t…Ē baki Kaisaro …Ēpani niŐĀ y…õ liboe le su. L…õ maki …ĒsaŐÉ si bakp…õ y…õ awla ki ban…Ēpe ba k…Ēz…õna ni lipoe l…õ kl…Ē gi maatani y…õ kutsisi Kaisaro kl…Ē …õ.‚ÄĚ

ref: Then Agrippa said to Festus, ‚ÄúI would also like to hear what this man has to say.‚ÄĚ Festus answered, ‚ÄúYou can hear him tomorrow.‚ÄĚ
text: Agripa asi Festo si, ‚ÄúMaap…õ si m…õ …Ētut…Ē minu …Ēnili…õ kl…Ē y…õ kunugu yo.‚ÄĚ L…õ Festo ebu le onu si, ‚ÄúW…Ēanu y…õ onu kivo.‚ÄĚ Paulo al…õ…õ ni Agripa AŇču m…õ

ref: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
text: Gi kelea k…õti i, Agripa ni Bernike b…õba niŐĀ sikusi me, l…õ banima b…õkp…õ kubuŐÄbuŐÄ glegle niŐĀ basu u, gi beetiku niŐĀ …Ētsakl…Ē …õ, baguta ba baka ba k…Ēp…Ēni …Ēma n…Ē ban…Ē  čidi ba beze ni ba kude. Festo aki …ĒsaŐÉ, l…õ b…õz…õmani Paulo e.

ref: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
text: Festo apani si, ‚ÄúOkusi  čidi ye Agripa ani banama p…ĒŐĀ gi b…õtsa niŐĀ ya ni bl…Ē …Ē: Dani ml…õm…Ē …Ēnili…õ ya, gi y…õ Yudan…õ ma p…ĒŐĀ …Ē gi bali niŐĀ ya ni Yerusalem i b…õtsaŐÄ y…õ onu kiŐĀ m…õ …õ. B…õkp…õ l…õ ozi si liŐĀma si oze ligbaŐÉŐĀ t…Ē …Ē.

ref: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
text: P…ĒŐÄ maŐĀm…Ē kesetata kat…Ēt…Ē niŐĀ y…õ su u, gi kiaki si biatsa kutseŐĀpia …Ēki y…õ …õ. P…ĒŐÄ gi y…õ …Ētut…Ē e či si batsini y…õ niŐĀ Kaisaro kl…Ē …õ, mazi l…õ si maatsisi y…õ.

ref: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
text: P…ĒŐÄ maŐĀm…Ē liboeboe e, gi maaŇčwlimi niŐĀ y…õ su tsisi bl…Ē Okusi  čidi ye Kaisaro o. L…õ loso mamani y…õ niŐĀ ml…Ē petee aŇču m…õ niŐĀ …Ēt…Ēpu k…Ē …õ Okusi  čidi ye Agripa at…Ē xe kwipami y…õ liboe le me p…ĒŐĀ …õ, maam…Ē liboet…Ē gi maaŇčwlimi i.

ref: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
text: L…õ ese linu si mam…Ē si biŐĀtratr…Ē m…õ a ča si mik…Ē …Ēn…Ē gi b…õkp…õ ligba tsisi y…õ …õ, xegi maŐĀŇčwlimi kidili…õ gi k…õ abit…õ …õ, xe k…õ loso bevu ye kp…õ ligba l…õ …õ.‚ÄĚ

ref: Agrippa told Paul, ‚ÄúYou may now speak for yourself.‚ÄĚ Paul stretched out his hand and said:
text: Agripa asi Paulo si, ‚ÄúKudeonu kwidra …õki w…Ē si bu isu ya onu.‚ÄĚ L…õ loso Paulo edo ye …Ėase l…õ ekpese isu ya onu kubu si:

ref: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
text: ‚ÄúOkusi  čidi ye Agripa! Mebu isu ya si m…õ litukpo lip…õ si omete e, matrabu isu ya onu niŐĀ w…Ē aŇču m…õ niŐĀ liboe kakaa gi Yudan…õ ma b…õk…Ē niŐĀ m…õ su i su.

ref: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
text: Li veve le linu si wote Yudan…õ ma ik…Ē l…õ petee ni ba eboe hehe la ni ba kiboebit…õ…õ. Mee či si ta m…õ eboe la kutu ni lit…Ē pl…õpl…õ kese.

ref: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
text: Bete lipoe kakaa, xe b…õp…õ si b…õpani niŐĀ m…õ su i, si d…Ēni okpese k…Ē ke e, meze …Ēn…Ē gi mevu kusumu yo ik…Ē l…õ m…õ …Ėii Ňčwisi Farisi…Ēn…õ kin…õ …õ.

ref: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
text: L…õ abla …õ, mal…õ…õ niŐĀ ya gi b…õxwa litsi ni me e, k…Ēkp…õ m…õ kudeonudidi niŐĀ kakapl…õa lia gi Aya aka apl…õ aki bl…Ē baka ba a,

ref: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
text: kakapl…õa lia …Ėii gi bl…Ē amade li…Ēf…Ē ni ta ča la beedi kudeonu ik…Ē si biam…Ē, gi beesumu Aya elesi ni kemize e. L…õ k…Ēkp…õ kudeonudidi uli…Ē ya loso e, Okusi  čidi ye Agripa, Yudan…õ ma beebu me lip…Ē!

ref: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
text: Kit…õ lil…õ gagla …õki ml…Ē banama gi mlal…õ ni ya a si mlazi Ňča si Aya iz…õy…Ē ni bekpo  ča na a?

ref: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
